audited_scope: fixture/planted/ only — this answer key and fixture/README.md sit outside the repo root handed to the skill and must never be visible to any worker
scoring: recall = must_find items recovered in the final registers, matched by mechanism (not wording), with severity >= each item's min_severity; expected_type applies when the finding lands in the code-error register — a claims-register row scores as a match with the equivalent claim type (e.g. estimation_specification for P-02/P-03, data_construction or sample_count for P-05/P-08, treatment_definition for P-11) when the mechanism matches; precision check = no decoy flagged; findings beyond must_find are NOT penalized except the decoys; conflict check = every expected_status_conflicts rule satisfied on its own terms (each rule states when it applies); re-score after ANY skeleton edit. Behavior tests added 2026-07-06: P-12 exercises the second-read recall sweep (a second independent error in a file already flagged by P-06 — recovery requires re-reading the flagged file), P-13 exercises cheap-check arithmetic recompute (must be flagged inconsistent, not left mapped), and P-14 exercises the blocked-visible-metadata discipline. P-14 dual-accept: satisfied by an inconsistent claim (severity >= 2) OR by a blocked claim whose Blocked Check records the 1-in-20 vs one-in-ten contradiction (in that branch the severity floor is waived); a silently-blocked row with no such note fails P-14. Failure-class plants added 2026-07-07 (P-15 through P-20, one per new mechanism, each in a domain fresh relative to the Floods package): each carries a failure_class tag the scorer reports per class. P-20 uses the same dual-accept branch logic as P-14 (inconsistent at severity >= 2, OR blocked with a Blocked Check that itself records the 15-km vs 25 km contradiction, severity floor waived). Artifact-layer checks (single-re-score layer per KTD-8, run by the scorer alongside the register score): the U2 parser artifact audit/_run/manifest_check.md must name pyproject.toml as a candidate finding; if the P-19 claim closes confirmed, the U4 anchoring advisory must have warned on its recheck-ledger row; if the P-20 row rests blocked, the U5 filename-parameter advisory must warn on it; the U1 conventions-artifact/b4-candidate check is INFORMATIVE only, never gate-settling (the b3c consolidation and grep are worker-dependent). Definition/use contract plant added 2026-07-08 (P-21, failure_class definition_use_contract): a derived consent_ok release flag whose comment states it covers two consent families (individual and community) is consumed by a `keep if` whose added `& consent == "individual"` conjunct silently drops the community family; the narrowed sample feeds the on-path published Table 1, so the severity floor of 2 is honest. Scored as a standard must_find (mechanism signature + qualifying row at severity >= 2), dual-accepted as a code-side sample_filter_or_flag_error or a claim-side data_construction row, and reported under its own definition_use_contract class in the per-class breakdown. D-03 adds a matched-shape detector precision decoy: do/analysis.do defines baseline_diag_ok and applies keep if baseline_diag_ok == 1 & wave == 1 inside preserve/restore for an explicitly baseline-only diagnostic. It must be emitted, mapped, ledger-dispositioned not_error, and absent as an issue row.

must_find.where: paper/paper.tex Results section vs artifacts/tab1.tex
must_find.mechanism: Prose reports a coefficient of 0.083; the shipped table artifact shows -0.038. Digit-level mismatch at reported precision.

must_find.where: paper Sec. 2 ('clustered at the village level') vs do/analysis.do vce(cluster household_id)
must_find.mechanism: Paper says village-level clustering; code clusters at the household level.

must_find.where: paper Sec. 2 ('weighted using the survey weights') vs do/analysis.do
must_find.mechanism: svy_weight exists in the data and the paper claims weighted regressions, but the regressions run unweighted.

must_find.where: do/analysis.do bootstrap block
must_find.mechanism: bootstrap with 200 replications and no `set seed` (and no stable sort) — results not reproducible.

must_find.where: do/build_panel.do `keep if waves < 2` vs paper Sec. 1 exclusion rule
must_find.mechanism: Paper excludes households with fewer than two waves; the filter keeps exactly those households (reversed condition).

must_find.where: py/make_figures.py reads output/panel_v2.csv
must_find.mechanism: build_panel.do writes output/panel.csv; the figure script reads a nonexistent panel_v2.csv, so Figure 1 cannot be regenerated.

must_find.where: do/analysis.do `$controls` (definition commented out)
must_find.mechanism: The controls global is only defined in a commented line, so regressions silently run without controls while the table claims 'Household controls: Yes'.

must_find.where: do/build_panel.do income conversion vs paper Sec. 1 ('thousands of local currency units')
must_find.mechanism: Comment and paper say thousands; the code divides by 100, a factor-of-10 unit error in reported income levels.

must_find.where: planted/README.md data table vs actual inputs consumed
must_find.mechanism: README declares data/rainfall_stations.csv as an input; the file does not exist in the package and no script reads it.

must_find.where: data/households.csv columns head_name, gps_lat, gps_lon
must_find.mechanism: Respondent names and household GPS coordinates ship in the public data file.

must_find.expected_type: treatment_or_event_timing_error (aggregation_or_unit_error also accepted: the mechanism is a wrong-baseline mean, which reviewers may defensibly type either way)
must_find.where: paper Sec. 1 ('long-run historical mean (the 1991--2020 climate normal)') vs do/build_panel.do rain_mean
must_find.mechanism: Paper defines the shock as the deviation from the village's long-run historical (1991-2020) mean; build_panel.do computes rain_mean as the mean of rain_mm over the two in-sample waves, so the shock is measured against a two-observation in-sample mean, not a long-run climate normal. Designed to split the reviews: the code line reads as a plausible 'village mean' on surface inspection, so a claims pass may confirm the definition while the code pass flags the wrong baseline.

must_find.where: py/make_figures.py legend vs the plotted column order
must_find.mechanism: make_figures.py plots income by shock status: groupby(['wave','shocked']).unstack() yields columns [0,1] = [non-shocked, shocked], plotted in that order, but ax.legend(['Shocked','Non-shocked']) reverses the labels, so the figure series are mislabeled. SECOND-READ TEST: this is a second, independent error in a file whose prominent first finding is the P-06 stale path (reads output/panel_v2.csv). A satisficing first pass logs P-06 and stops; recovering P-12 requires re-reading the already-flagged file. Behavior-shaped (two-independent-errors-in-one-file), not floods-specific.

must_find.where: paper/paper.tex Data section: '725 households --- 25 percent of the panel'
must_find.mechanism: The prose reports 2,416 households and states 725 of them (25 percent) experienced a negative rainfall shock; 725/2,416 = 30 percent, not 25. CHEAP-CHECK TEST: a one-line arithmetic recompute against numbers already in the prose settles it -- no external data or script needed -- so it must be flagged inconsistent, not left 'mapped'/located-but-unverified. Behavior-shaped (paper arithmetic slip), not floods-specific.

must_find.where: paper/paper.tex Data section ('public one-in-ten subsample') vs planted/README.md households.csv description ('public 1-in-20 subsample')
must_find.mechanism: The paper says estimates use a one-in-ten subsample and that the complete enumeration is restricted-access, so a naive check marks the claim blocked (the full census cannot be inspected). But the visible package metadata already contradicts it: the README describes data/households.csv as a '1-in-20 subsample'. BLOCKED-VISIBLE TEST: a visible-metadata check on a blocked claim (filenames, headers, README descriptions) catches the one-in-ten vs 1-in-20 contradiction. Dual-accept: recovered as an inconsistent claim (preferred), OR as a blocked claim whose Blocked Check explicitly records the 1-in-20 contradiction. A silently-blocked row with no Blocked Check note is a FAILURE of this test. Behavior-shaped (blocked claim whose visible metadata contradicts it), not floods-specific.

must_find.where: paper Data section four-component income list vs py/build_income.py components
must_find.mechanism: The paper enumerates the four income components (crop sales, livestock sales, wage earnings, remittances); build_income.py hand-retypes the list as three, omitting remittances, so the component aggregate silently drops a stated member. U1 TEST (enumerated_member_list): the paper-stated list is claims-visible and qualifies for b3c consolidation under the single-row carve-out; the divergent re-materialization is the b4 set-difference grep's target. Fresh domain (income components), no Floods category list.
must_find.recovery_path: b3c enumerated_member_list consolidation (single-row carve-out) -> b4 conventions grep set-difference -> candidate resolved at recheck; the conventions-artifact check in the scorer is INFORMATIVE only (worker-dependent per KTD-8)

must_find.where: pyproject.toml (version = 0.4.1, unquoted)
must_find.mechanism: pyproject.toml is invalid TOML — `version = 0.4.1` is an unquoted value every real TOML parser rejects — so the README's documented `pip install -e .` cannot parse the manifest. U2 TEST (manifest parseability): a different format and ecosystem from the Floods requirements-operator defect; check_manifests.py must reject it and name it in audit/_run/manifest_check.md, and the b4 fold-in must mint the candidate.

must_find.where: do/build_panel.do hhsize backfill guard
must_find.mechanism: The comment says the line backfills MISSING household size, but `if hhsize < .` is Stata's NON-missing test, so the replace acts only where hhsize is already present and the planted missing value (household 105, wave 2) is never filled. U3 TEST (empirical verification, repair-guard idiom): the comment primes a reader past the wrong condition; a two-row synthetic probe with one missing value settles it. Planted in a file already carrying unrelated plants (P-05, P-08, P-11). Fresh idiom and variable (hhsize, `< .`), not the Floods repair guard.

must_find.where: py/build_income.py has_wages loop
must_find.mechanism: The comment says has_wages flags households reporting wage earnings in ANY wave, but the loop assigns (rather than accumulates) the column each iteration, so the wave-2 pass erases wave-1 matches and the final flag reflects the last iteration only. U3 TEST (loop-flag idiom): probing two iterations, one matching and one not, settles it. Planted in a file also carrying the unrelated P-15 and P-19 plants. Fresh variable and idiom, not the Floods match flag. min_severity lowered 2->1 on 2026-07-08: the flag feeds only the off-path diagnostic output/income_check.csv, so under the registers.md author-materiality rubric a correct rating is severity 1; the floor of 2 conflicted with the skill's own rubric (gate run 1 scorecard).

must_find.expected_type: estimation_specification or data_construction claim (code-side aggregation_or_unit_error also accepted)
must_find.where: paper Sec. 2 wage_earnings winsorisation vs py/build_income.py
must_find.mechanism: The paper says wage earnings (wage_earnings) are winsorised at the 99th percentile; build_income.py implements a 99th-percentile winsorisation on crop_sales instead — the described operation exists, on a DIFFERENT variable. U4 TEST (identifier anchoring): confirming the claim because a 99th-percentile winsorisation exists anchors the operation, not the named identifier; the claim must not close confirmed. Fresh domain (income-component winsorisation), not the Floods variable surface.

must_find.where: paper Appendix A step 2 (15-km gauge radius) vs data/village_rain_radius_25km.csv
must_find.mechanism: Appendix A enumerates a three-step rainfall construction; step 2 states gauges are matched within a 15-km radius, but the shipped village series is named village_rain_radius_25km.csv — the filename encodes 25 km. The match itself is upstream (gauge coordinates not shipped), so a naive check blocks the claim while the shipped FILENAME already contradicts it. U5 TEST (per-step extraction + filename-parameter reconciliation): the parameter lives in step 2 of an enumerated procedure, so a single-row extraction of the whole procedure loses it; extends the P-14 blocked-visible family. Dual-accept: inconsistent claim (severity >= 2) OR blocked claim whose Blocked Check records the 15-km vs 25 km contradiction (severity floor waived); a silently-blocked row fails. Fresh parameter kind (distance radius, not return periods).

must_find.where: do/build_panel.do consent_ok flag definition vs its keep-if consumer
must_find.mechanism: The consent_ok flag's adjacent comment states it covers BOTH consent routes — explicit individual data-sharing consent AND village-level blanket (community) consent, both approved for release — but the estimation-sample filter `keep if consent_ok == 1 & consent == "individual"` adds a conjunct that silently drops the community-consent households, so the published Table 1 sample excludes a case family the flag is defined to cover. DEFINITION/USE CONTRACT TEST: the producer-defined coverage (two consent families) is compared against the consumer's effective predicate (individual only); the extra conjunct narrows the covered set with no independently defined eligibility restriction and no companion consumer recovering the community family. The narrowed sample flows to output/panel.dta -> analysis.do -> artifacts/tab1.tex (on-path), so the severity floor of 2 is honest under the author-materiality rubric. Planted in do/build_panel.do, already carrying the unrelated P-05/P-08/P-11/P-17 plants (the shape of the real misses). Fresh domain (survey consent/release flag gating a published table); the excluded family is a substantively distinct case family named in the comment, not a missing-value idiom, so it shares no surface features with the Floods repair guard or with P-15..P-20.

must_find.where: requirements-recall.txt line 1
must_find.mechanism: The requirements manifest uses whitespace between numpy and 2.0.0 instead of a legal version operator, so a real requirements parser rejects the dependency declaration. U6a PLANT A: check_manifests.py must emit it, the detector mapping must carry its candidate row, and the second-read plan must select the file with reason detector.

must_find.where: requirements-recall.txt lines 2-3
must_find.mechanism: The same otherwise-clean requirements file pins pandas to two mutually incompatible exact versions, 2.2.2 and 1.5.3. Each line parses alone, so the manifest emitter does not detect this semantic conflict; a whole-file human second read must surface it. U6a PLANT B.

must_find.where: paper/paper.tex body prose ('calculated and reference speeds show substantial overlap') vs appendix Figure A2 and py/make_figures.py
must_find.mechanism: The body prose says calculated and reference speeds show substantial overlap and points to Figure A2, while make_figures.py plots calculated speeds 8-11 and reference speeds 28-34 in separate appendix panels — disjoint ranges. U7a CLAIM-HANDOFF TEST: the body sentence and appendix figure must execute under different claims workers; the deterministic printed-reference inventory independently assigns the body assertion even though the caption only labels Panel A and Panel B.

must_find.where: sh/ac_master.sh passes two positional arguments through a macroized Julia command to jl/ac_e1201.jl
must_find.mechanism: The master shell script passes public and private as two positional arguments to the Julia callee through a placeholder-root path and --threads option, but the callee reads only ARGS[1], so argpos:2 is silently ignored. The fully-consuming Python call in the same master script is the zero-flag control.

must_not_find.mechanism: A fully commented-out figure references artifacts/fig_placebo.pdf, which no script produces. Comment-blanking removes it from the audited paper; any finding about the placebo figure or fig_placebo.pdf is a false positive.

farm_components = ['crop_sales', 'livestock_sales'] materializes a deliberate, locally-signposted SUBSET of the paper's four-component income list (the comment declares it a farm-only descriptive share). The U1 intentional-subset guard must record the site reviewed-not-divergent; a SUBSET-OMISSION complaint about the farm components or farm share (that the list omits/diverges from the four-component definition) is a false positive. Narrowed 2026-07-08: other findings inside the block (e.g. the true zero-income division guard observation) are scored on their own merits and do not trip the decoy.

The intentionally baseline-wave-only diagnostic defines baseline_diag_ok, then uses keep if baseline_diag_ok == 1 & wave == 1 inside preserve/restore before summarize log_income. The shape must be emitted by the definition/use detector, but recheck must clear it not_error because wave == 1 is the diagnostic's explicitly stated domain and restore prevents any change to the estimation sample.

expected_status_conflicts.rule: Conditional — applies only when the run both (a) leaves a claims-register row asserting the long-run-mean shock definition at status confirmed AND (b) records a confirmed code-error row for the P-11 mechanism. In that case the cross-linker must link the pair, list it under '## Status conflicts' in register_cross_link_summary.md, and the conflict must be resolved before b8 (the claim must not remain confirmed while linked to the confirmed error). If instead the claims review directly marks the shock-definition claim inconsistent or confirmation_needed, no conflict arises and this rule passes vacuously — P-11 recall is required either way. A run that has both (a) and (b) but never surfaces the conflict fails this check even if P-11 recall passes.

expected_confirmed_examples: N = 4,832 in prose matches the artifact table (transcription check that should pass)

The README's per-object script mapping is correct: no readme_or_package_mismatch finding about the Table 1 -> analysis.do or Figure 1 -> make_figures.py mapping rows (the P-06 stale-path finding on make_figures.py itself is expected and separate)
